notes: Forward returns at 5/20/60d horizons overlap across consecutive daily signals; effective independent sample is far below nominal n. Directional evidence only — not grounds for mechanical parameter changes.
v8 telemetry (divergence, hostile_review, regime driver/basis) began accruing mid-June 2026; sections 2-4 deepen with time.
Temporal z baseline excludes the most recent observation; generate-signals consumes the stored baseline next morning (one-day staleness, immaterial at window 60).

tactical.falsifier: A sustained break below the $260 52w low on rising volume, or cohort lag widening past 15pp with a negative catalyst, would flip this from buy-the-dip to falling-knife (positive score).

positional.falsifier: A B/B reading below 0.95 with negative backlog YoY, or DoD budget shifting to CR/sequester risk, would confirm the down-trend is fundamental and flip the sign positive.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmed forward P/E moving to a premium vs cohort (>105%) combined with EPS revisions turning negative >-1% over 90d would validate a genuine trim (larger positive score); conversely a margin recovery to 13%+ would push this negative.

tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking below 35 with follow-through selling on confirmed negative news (e.g., a major foundry capex cut announcement) would flip this to a positive/de-risking score.

positional.falsifier: A weekly close below the 200-day SMA ($1356.86) or a book-to-bill dropping below 1.0 would flip the positional read to trim/positive.

strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E climbing above 45x OR a book-to-bill printing below 1.0 with declining backlog would push this decisively to trim; conversely a >15% drawdown taking forward P/E under 25x would flip it negative.

tactical.falsifier: QUAL vs SPY flow flipping negative (>1pp outflow) alongside LIN breaking below the 50-day SMA ($514) on volume would flip this positive.

positional.falsifier: LIN/APD spread widening past +3pp (unjustified premium expansion) OR a decisive break below the 200-day ($472) would flip this positive.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that ROCE erosion is structural (below 22% on a clean invested-capital basis) with price/mix ex-FX going negative — genuine moat erosion, not a goodwill artifact — would push this firmly higher; conversely a >$1B H2 project win or backlog acceleration would flip it negative.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive break below the 52w low of $349.20 on rising volume, or RSI rolling under 40, would flip this from a dip-buy to a broken-trend trim.

positional.falsifier: Azure constant-currency growth decelerating below 22% on the next print, or a hyperscaler capex blowout with margin compression, would confirm thesis impairment and flip positional to trim.

strategic.falsifier: An OpenAI partnership unwind or unfavorable renegotiation, or forward P/E expanding above 35x with cohort premium flipping positive on stalling Azure, would flip the strategic sign to trim.

tactical.falsifier: RSI spiking above 75 or a sharp gap to the 52w high on volume would flip this to a genuine tactical trim; a break below the 50-day SMA would flip it to a buy-the-dip.

positional.falsifier: A break below the 200-day SMA ($51.37) or a dividend-coverage scare (DCF payout creeping above 70%) would flip this decisively negative-for-price (trim confirmed); conversely a 10Y drop below ~4.0% widening the spread past ~90bps would flip me to a buy.

strategic.falsifier: Rate cuts driving the 10Y sustainably below 4% (spread normalizes, re-rating) would flip me negative-for-score (buy); conversely a Mainline recontracting setback, LNG project delay, or dividend-growth pause would flip me to a firmer trim.

tactical.falsifier: A reclaim of the 50-day ($14.90) on rising volume, or LME copper reversing higher on Chinese stimulus, would flip this to a tactical buy.

positional.falsifier: GLNCY breaking and closing below the 200-day SMA, or the COPX spread flipping negative (GLNCY underperforming pure copper), would flip the sign positive.

strategic.falsifier: Evidence the copper cycle has peaked rather than troughed — sustained LME copper decline, rising exchange inventories, and Chinese demand contraction — plus GLNCY P/E collapsing toward single digits on a peak-earnings basis, would flip the sign to trim.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive break below the $32.84 52w low on rising volume would flip this positive (trim), confirming momentum breakdown rather than a bounce setup.

positional.falsifier: Sustained ETF net outflows for 2+ weeks combined with a break below the $32.84 52w low would flip me positive — confirming the cycle-break/distribution thesis rather than an accumulation zone.

strategic.falsifier: Evidence of structural demand destruction — sustained multi-quarter ETF net outflows or major regulatory reversal — would push strategic firmly negative-thesis (sell); conversely a new all-time high with expanding flows would push it clearly buy.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close above the 50-day ($106.1) on strong volume (flip negative) or a break below the 200-day ($98.97) with peso weakening past 18.5 (flip positive).

positional.falsifier: MXN breaking weaker past ~18.5 on tariff escalation or a Banxico dovish surprise, plus a break below the 200-day — would flip positive. Conversely a clean reclaim of the 50-day would deepen the buy.

strategic.falsifier: A sustained MXN depreciation regime (break and hold above ~19-20/USD driven by tariffs or fiscal stress) or a structural volume decline in Mexico would flip this positive.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive RSI break above 65 with sustained close above the 50-day ($18.2), or conversely a break below the 200-day ($15.93) on rising volume.

positional.falsifier: WTI breaking below ~$70 or a fuel-pricing/dividend-policy headline that structurally impairs the payout would flip this positive (trim).

strategic.falsifier: A concrete government action cutting the dividend policy or mandating large uneconomic investment (or WTI collapse toward breakeven) would flip strategic to positive/trim.

tactical.falsifier: A daily close below the 50-day SMA ($12.30) on rising volume would flip the tactical read from mildly-trim to buy-the-dip.

positional.falsifier: GSCPI dropping below 0.5 or WCI breaking below full-cycle breakeven (~$1,500/FEU) would flip the freight tailwind to a headwind and turn this layer negative-to-trim... i.e., sign flips positive/trim decisively.

strategic.falsifier: A sustained collapse in global trade volumes (CPB World Trade Monitor turning negative YoY) combined with the orderbook flooding capacity would flip strategic to trim/positive despite the P/B discount, as trough earnings deepen into losses.

tactical.falsifier: A daily close back below SMA50 (~$484) on weak volume with XBI 30d rolling negative would flip this to a trim/neutral posture.

positional.falsifier: XBI 90d rolling back negative (<-10%, refreezing) combined with TMO breaking below SMA200 (~$529) would flip the positional sign to trim.

strategic.falsifier: Simultaneous China capex collapse and confirmed NIH funding cuts, or a Q1/Q2 2026 print showing organic growth stalling below ~2% with guidance cut, would flip the strategic sign to trim.

tactical.falsifier: A break and hold below SMA50 ($103.37) with RSI rolling under 40 on rising volume would flip this to a momentum-down (positive/trim) tactical read.

positional.falsifier: A negative 30/60/90d EPS revision trend or a cRPO print decelerating below 18% would flip positional to trim (positive).

strategic.falsifier: Real rate breaking above 2% sustained, OR a confirmed multi-quarter cRPO deceleration below 18%, would push strategic decisively positive (trim); conversely a cRPO re-acceleration disclosure would flip it clearly negative.

tactical.falsifier: A drawdown extending past -12% from the 52w high (breaking below ~$530 decisively / SMA200) would flip this to a tactical buy setup; conversely a fresh new 52w high on fading breadth would confirm the trim lean.

positional.falsifier: Cross-border volume printing <6% or a negative 90d EPS revision inflection at the next earnings report would flip the positional sign to trim; a break above the 52w high on strong volume would reinforce the buy.

strategic.falsifier: Measurable real-economy merchant stablecoin displacement OR interchange/network-fee legislation advancing to a floor vote (moving from stalled to advancing/passed) would flip strategic to trim; twin premium blowing out past 25% would also erase the valuation edge.

tactical.falsifier: If the July 16 transcript shows the 2027 transition quantified as material (>mid-single-digit % of revenue) OR a procedure-guide cut with Hugo/Ottava share-loss evidence, the sign flips to trim; if instead procedures beat guide and transition stayed unquantified/manageable, conviction increases toward -64.

positional.falsifier: A confirmed negative turn in 30d/90d EPS revisions post-July-16 (estimates cut on transition/margin) flips this toward neutral/trim; continued positive revisions with the stock this stretched keeps max conviction.

strategic.falsifier: Management quantifying the 2027 transition as MATERIAL on the July 16 call (or measurable US procedure-share loss to Hugo/Ottava) flips this to trim; quantifying it as manageable or leaving it benign with procedures beating guide pushes toward strong buy.
